---
title: "Theoretical and Empirical Research Methodology"
subtitle: "Fall semester 2026"
---

# 📑 The course in brief

**Focus:**
This is the course website for the module *Theoretical and Empirical Research
Methodology* in the Master *International Management Studies* at the
Europa-Universität Flensburg. The module has two complementary parts:

- the **[main lecture](/content/lecture/)** (Mondays), which introduces the
  theoretical and methodological foundations of empirical research — from logic
  and argumentation, over qualitative methods such as interviews and case
  studies, to quantitative tools such as regression analysis and experiments;
- the **[implementation lab](/content/material/)** (Fridays), which puts these
  concepts into practice by teaching you the statistical programming language R
  and how to implement the essential research tools discussed in the lecture.

This website hosts the material for **both** parts so that lecture and lab are
easy to navigate together.

**How:**
The course comprises a mixture of (i) lectures, in which concepts are introduced
in the classroom, (ii) on-site and video labs for the practical R work, (iii)
automated hands-on exercises for you to do at home, and (iv) instructional
videos for topics that are best learned by yourself. You are always invited to
post your questions to the course forum on Moodle, and more intricate problems
can be solved in class.

**Prerequisites:**
The lab does not require you to have any prior knowledge in R or any other
programming language. Depending on your prior knowledge or affinity to
programming, the course will be quite demanding, but it equips you with
computational skills that are most valuable both within academia and the
business world.

# 🎯 Learning Objectives

In the **implementation lab** you will learn to:

-	Use R together with the integrated development environment R Studio
-	Understand the use of R packages to perform specific data analytic tasks
-	Write reproducible data analysis reports using Quarto
-	Transform raw data into tidy data, which is suitable for further analysis
-	Choose and justify the correct visualization approach, and create appealing visualizations using the R package `ggplot2`
-	Implement and interpret linear regression models with numerical and categorial variables
- Analyze experimental data in R

The **main lecture** complements these practical skills with the conceptual and
methodological foundations needed to design, conduct and critically assess
empirical research.


-----------------


<a href=https://elearning.uni-flensburg.de/moodle/course/view.php?id=17920>
    <button class="button-61">The main course on Moodle</button>
</a>
